title: Switzerland Data Residency Baseline (revFADP/nDSG)
notes: Constrains placement to Swiss cloud regions and evidences the revised Swiss Federal Act on Data Protection (revFADP / nDSG, in force 1 Sept 2023). Evidence of technical/organizational controls, not a cryptographic data-path proof.

rules.name: Workloads pinned to a Swiss region
rules.description: Every workload declares node placement constrained to an allow-listed Swiss cloud region (Zurich/Geneva) via topology.kubernetes.io/region.
rules.rationale: Cross-border disclosure of personal data is only permitted to states with adequate protection or under specific safeguards; keeping workloads in Switzerland avoids the transfer question.

articles.title: Cross-border disclosure of personal data — principles

rules.name: No unrestricted or cross-border egress
rules.description: Services do not proxy to external endpoints (ExternalName) and NetworkPolicies do not permit unrestricted (0.0.0.0/0, ::/0) egress.
rules.rationale: Open egress can disclose personal data across the border without meeting the FADP conditions.

articles.title: Cross-border disclosure of personal data — principles

rules.name: Storage uses customer-managed keys
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims reference a customer-managed key (CMK) so the controller controls the keys.
rules.rationale: Customer-managed keys are an appropriate technical measure to ensure data security.

rules.name: Encryption at rest declared
rules.description: PersistentVolumeClaims declare encryption at rest via the configured label/annotation.
rules.rationale: Encryption at rest is an appropriate technical measure to ensure data security.
